summary: omnius ships a postinstall daemon that gathers host info and beacons over HTTP
details: The package's lifecycle hooks declared in package.json (preinstall and postinstall, with curl invocations on line 142) trigger a Node-based daemon at dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs. That file pulls in child_process, os, fs, and http; calls os.userInfo() and execSync() to collect host identity and run shell commands; uses ping to probe network reachability; and issues outbound http.request GET/POST traffic. The combination of automatic install-time execution, system-information collection (user identity, command output), and outbound HTTP makes this a host-beaconing/exfiltration shape rather than a build or native-addon pattern — there is no shipped C/C++ source, no node-gyp build, and no publisher-matched CDN fetch. A secondary scripts/web_scrape.py script in the tarball performs further network probing (wget/ping/POST), reinforcing the install-time data-collection profile. Any machine running `npm install omnius` will execute this daemon and contact the hardcoded host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
